title: Why doesnt Eva go into other archetypes
display_title: Why doesnt Eva go into other archetypes
wikitext:
[]: Why doesn't Eva go into other archetypes?             Quote Post by GAP » Wed Dec 08, 2010 10:50 pm I wonder why doesdn't Eva deeper into other archetypes in Sper Robots and shounen in general? There is the mad scientist dad Gendo, bottle fairy Misato, Tsundere Asuka, Kuudere Rei and even the the team pet Pen Pen. Why no hot blooded heroes, the stoic rival or love interest?

[]: Re: Why doesn't Eva go into other archetypes?             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 08, 2010 10:52 pm GAP wrote: Why no hot blooded heroes , the stoic rival or love interest ?

[]: Re: Why doesn't Eva go into other archetypes?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 08, 2010 11:20 pm Sachi wrote: You seem to be describing Asuka in all of these, but mostly the bolded.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Dec 09, 2010 8:29 am Tsundere Asuka,

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 09, 2010 9:01 am Jayfive wrote: In Rebuild maybe, but not in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 09, 2010 11:00 am I always thought that Evangelion had more in common with Sentai & Tokusatsu (Kamen Rider, Ultraman, Super Sentai) than with mecha (Gundam, Ideon, Maccross).

[]: Re: Why doesn't Eva go into other archetypes?             Quote Post by Lucretius » Thu Dec 09, 2010 12:59 pm GAP wrote: I wonder why doesdn't Eva deeper into other archetypes in Sper Robots and shounen in general? There is the mad scientist dad Gendo, bottle fairy Misato, Tsundere Asuka, Kuudere Rei and even the the team pet Pen Pen. Why no hot blooded heroes, the stoic rival or love interest?

[]:             Quote Post by Galaxy News Reidio » Thu Dec 09, 2010 12:59 pm Quite frankly, why should it? It would be damn hard to get every single trope, cliche and archetype into a 26-episode show.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Thu Dec 09, 2010 2:29 pm Jayfive wrote: Really? Ive been meaning to bring up Asuka been labelled as this. Definition of Tsundere: "describes a person who is initially cold and even hostile towards another person before gradually showing their warm side over time. " In Rebuild maybe, but not in NGE. IMO No way no how. At what point does she ever do anything remotely nice (veiled by insults or not) to Shinji that isnt motivated out of selfishness? Hell she even goads him into steaming in against Leliel and then doesnt show the least concern for him when he comes a cropper.

[]: Re: Why doesn't Eva go into other archetypes?             Quote Post by Aozora » Thu Dec 09, 2010 2:34 pm Lucretius wrote: Only on egf is stringing together a bunch of cliches and slightly tweaking them considered great art.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Thu Dec 09, 2010 4:20 pm I shot myself in the foot with this, I just realized that both Asuka and Rei were exactly the archetypes I was talking about. Man, I should had known that the whole time and I wasted a good thread.

[]: Re: Why doesn't Eva go into other archetypes?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Dec 09, 2010 5:28 pm Lucretius wrote: Only on egf is stringing together a bunch of cliches and slightly tweaking them considered great art.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Dec 10, 2010 2:10 am It's also called "Watchmen". "Tweaking" stereotypes in order to explore what's behind them CAN be great art, and there's a grand tradition of doing just that.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 10, 2010 4:18 pm The "stoic Rival" would be Rei (to Asuka) even if Asuka has some elements of the ace-ish ambitious archrival - Its just that Shinji is not the type for Rivalry. If he was Naruto, he'd probably just be emo for a while after Sasuke's departure and then try to forget the guy. (OMG, Shinji as Naruto... That wouldn't work on so many ways.... ) If Asuka isn't hot blooded hero-ish enough, try Mari, tough none of them fit the cliché completely. (Asuka's too concerned with appearing superior, Mari has too much misplaced serenity) I guess Touji could count as one if he'd stayed an EVA pilot for longer. Him and Kensuke always struck me as being two of the traditional hero archetypes - Kensuke as the classic big dreamer, Touji as the hot-headed by honest one. But they just don't get 'chosen' or don't get any heroic beginner's luck.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Fri Dec 10, 2010 9:59 pm We all know what happened in Episode 15 (or was it 16?) and how Touji ended up. Sadly, he pretty is dead in the manga while the anime was a lot more merciful in that regard.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Dec 10, 2010 10:41 pm GAP wrote: We all know what happened in Episode 15 (or was it 16?) and how Touji ended up. Sadly, he pretty is dead in the manga while the anime was a lot more merciful in that regard.

[]:             Quote Post by Born of Lilith » Fri Dec 10, 2010 11:31 pm Honestly, I doubt "going into archetypes" was ever a big focus of Anno's to begin with, maybe an afterthought at most.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sat Dec 11, 2010 11:37 pm NGE =/= Pop Art. Stop this madness.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 22, 2010 7:50 am ran1 wrote: NGE =/= Pop Art.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Dec 22, 2010 8:31 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Like most of the best art.
summary:
[]: Why doesn't Eva go into other archetypes?             Quote Post by GAP » Wed Dec 08, 2010 10:50 pm I wonder why doesdn't Eva deeper into other archetypes in Sper Robots and shounen in general? There is the mad scientist dad Gendo, bottle fairy Misato, Tsundere Asuka, Kuudere Rei and even the the team pet Pen Pen. Why no hot blooded heroes, the stoic rival or love interest?

[]: Re: Why doesn't Eva go into other archetypes?  &n
